questions.q: What does the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad' mean in English?
questions.explanation: 'Inquilab Zindabad' translates as 'Long Live the Revolution'.

questions.q: Which revolutionary made the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad' most famous?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh and his comrades raised 'Inquilab Zindabad' most famously during their protests and trial.

questions.q: The word 'Inquilab' in the slogan refers to what?
questions.explanation: 'Inquilab' is the Urdu and Hindustani word for revolution.

questions.q: The word 'Zindabad' in the slogan means what?
questions.explanation: 'Zindabad' means 'long live' or 'may it live long'.

questions.q: 'Inquilab Zindabad' is drawn primarily from which language?
questions.explanation: The slogan is an Urdu/Hindustani phrase that became a pan-Indian rallying cry.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt shouted 'Inquilab Zindabad' while throwing bombs in which 1929 incident?
questions.explanation: The slogan rang out as Bhagat Singh and Dutt threw bombs in the Central Legislative Assembly in April 1929.

questions.q: During the freedom struggle, 'Inquilab Zindabad' captured what spirit?
questions.explanation: The slogan expressed the spirit of defiance and revolution against colonial rule.

questions.q: The slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad' is associated most closely with which group of revolutionaries?
questions.explanation: It became a battle cry of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association revolutionaries.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh's comrade who shouted the slogan with him in the Assembly was who?
questions.explanation: Batukeshwar Dutt was with Bhagat Singh in the Assembly and raised the slogan alongside him.

questions.q: 'Inquilab Zindabad' functioned during the freedom movement as a what?
questions.explanation: It served as a rallying cry that inspired nationalists across the country.

questions.q: The phrase 'Inquilab Zindabad' is widely credited to have been coined by which poet?
questions.explanation: The Urdu poet and freedom fighter Hasrat Mohani is generally credited with coining the slogan.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh and Dutt threw bombs in the Assembly mainly to protest which legislative measures?
options: The Public Safety and Trade Disputes Bills
questions.explanation: They acted against the Public Safety Bill and the Trade Disputes Bill, throwing bombs to 'make the deaf hear'.

questions.q: When throwing the bombs, Bhagat Singh and Dutt also showered the Assembly with what?
questions.explanation: They scattered leaflets explaining that the bombs were a symbolic protest, not meant to kill.

questions.q: After throwing the bombs, what did Bhagat Singh and Dutt deliberately do?
questions.explanation: They chose not to escape, courting arrest so they could use the trial as a political platform.

questions.q: For Bhagat Singh, the 'revolution' in 'Inquilab Zindabad' meant more than just what?
options: Merely the use of bombs and pistols
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh clarified that revolution did not mean only armed force, but a transformation of the social and economic order.

questions.q: The Assembly bombing in which 'Inquilab Zindabad' rang out took place in which city?
questions.explanation: The Central Legislative Assembly was in Delhi, where the 1929 bombing took place.

questions.q: The slogan is often paired with which complementary cry against imperialism?
options: Samrajyavad ka Naash Ho (Down with Imperialism)
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh and his comrades often paired 'Inquilab Zindabad' with 'Samrajyavad ka Naash Ho' (Down with Imperialism).

questions.q: Hasrat Mohani, credited with the slogan, was also notable for being among the first to demand what?
questions.explanation: Hasrat Mohani was among the earliest leaders to call for complete independence rather than Dominion status.

questions.q: The Assembly action where the slogan was raised was carried out under the banner of which organisation?
questions.explanation: The bombing was an action of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).

questions.q: 'Inquilab Zindabad' continued to inspire later mass movements, including which 1942 campaign?
questions.explanation: The slogan remained a popular cry through later phases, including the Quit India Movement of 1942.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh insisted the bombs thrown in the Assembly were designed to do what?
options: Make a loud protest without killing
questions.explanation: The low-intensity bombs were thrown away from people, intended to make noise and a statement rather than to kill.

questions.q: In which year did the Central Legislative Assembly bombing accompanied by 'Inquilab Zindabad' take place?
questions.explanation: The bombing occurred on 8 April 1929.

questions.q: Hasrat Mohani, credited with coining the slogan, hailed from which town in the United Provinces?
questions.explanation: Hasrat Mohani took his name from Mohan, a town in the Unnao district of the United Provinces.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh elaborated his idea of 'inquilab' most fully in which famous courtroom or written statement?
options: His statement during the Assembly bomb trial
questions.explanation: In his statement during the Assembly bomb case, Bhagat Singh explained that revolution meant overthrowing an unjust order, not mere bloodshed.

questions.q: Which leftist publication and ideal did Bhagat Singh link to the spirit of 'Inquilab'?
options: A revival of the Mughal empire
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh tied 'inquilab' to a socialist transformation, aiming to end exploitation of man by man.

questions.q: The slogan gained wider currency after which 1928-29 events involving the HSRA?
options: The Saunders killing and the Assembly bombing
questions.explanation: Public attention to the slogan grew sharply after the 1928 Saunders killing and the 1929 Assembly bombing trials.

questions.q: During the trial, Bhagat Singh and his comrades repeatedly used the courtroom to do what?
questions.explanation: They turned the courtroom into a stage, raising 'Inquilab Zindabad' and spreading their political message.

questions.q: Batukeshwar Dutt, who shouted the slogan with Bhagat Singh, received what sentence for the Assembly bombing?
questions.explanation: Dutt was sentenced to transportation for life to the Cellular Jail, unlike Bhagat Singh who was later hanged in a separate case.

questions.q: The leaflet thrown in the Assembly bore the name of which underground body?
questions.explanation: The leaflet was issued in the name of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (Army).

questions.q: Which line, popular among the revolutionaries who raised the slogan, comes from a verse linked to Ram Prasad Bismil?
questions.explanation: The verse 'Sarfaroshi ki Tamanna', associated with Bismil, was sung alongside cries of 'Inquilab Zindabad' by the revolutionaries.
